directive.title: Route Authorization Middleware for User Roles

auditor.summary: The build resolves all 7 prior findings and implements the core authorization middleware with proper role-based access control, audit denial logging, and performance benchmarks. However, two HIGH-severity issues remain: (1) the middleware awaits auditDenial() calls instead of using fire-and-forget pattern as required by the spec, and (2) the session.ts file contains a mock implementation with hardcoded credentials and no actual auth providers. Additionally, the test coverage is incomplete for full middleware handler integration tests. The build is close to passing but requires these critical fixes before shipping.

top_issues.description: The middleware awaits auditDenial() calls, which violates the spec requirement that auditDenial() must NEVER be awaited on the middleware critical path. The spec explicitly states 'auditDenial() must NEVER be awaited on the middleware critical path — use waitUntil or fire-and-forget keepalive fetch'. Currently, the middleware uses 'await auditDenial(...)' which blocks the response until the fetch completes.

The session.ts file contains a mock user database with hardcoded credentials and no actual auth providers configured. This is not a production-ready implementation and the spec requires the JWT encode callback to embed the role claim. The file has empty providers array which will cause next-auth to fail at runtime.

The ROUTE_RULES has a catch-all pattern /^\/api\/.*/ that matches ALL API routes including /api/internal/audit-denial which is the audit endpoint itself. This could cause the middleware to block the audit denial endpoint or create a circular dependency where the audit endpoint triggers another permission check.
